scope: Hard rule 'KuCoin spot agents cannot short' on KC-spot path only — does NOT supersede the spot-rejection rule itself, which remains in force at the KC execution gate
corrected_assertion: Shorting is NOW AUTHORIZED via a 3-stage progression on a separate Binance Futures path. Stage 1 (SHADOW): services/mae-futures-short/ writes shadow_short_positions audit rows only, NO venue calls — runs ≥14 days clean parity. Stage 2 (testnet paper): switch shadow service to Binance Futures testnet, $0 real exposure, validate fill semantics + liquidation handling for ≥7 days. Stage 3 (real-money canary): $10 positions on Binance Futures perps mainnet, max 3 concurrent, gated on (a) Plutus perps fine-tune COMPLETE, (b) operator capital ≥ $2K total, (c) 14-day clean SHADOW parity. The KC-spot hard rule remains in force on agents a5/a6/a8 — that rejection path is unchanged. Shorting capability lives ONLY on the new Binance Futures path with all three gates green.
source: GH Project #31 items #219-#223 [GCP-CUTOVER · Shorts] + operator decision moment 2026-05-13 in session 24cbed9c
retention_policy: permanent — re-evaluate on capital threshold change OR Plutus perps fine-tune drift >5%
